heading: Why Watermarks Are Important for Creators
text: Watermarks serve as a visible signature on your creative work, deterring unauthorized use and ensuring proper attribution. For photographers, designers, and content creators who share their work online, watermarks are a simple but effective way to protect intellectual property without restricting visibility.

heading: Types of Watermarks and When to Use Each
text: Text watermarks are the simplest and most common type. They typically include the creator's name, website URL, or copyright notice. Text watermarks are easy to create, highly customizable, and work well for photographers and bloggers who want straightforward attribution.

heading: Watermark Placement and Design Best Practices
text: Position your watermark where it cannot be easily cropped out. The center of the image is the most secure position, but it can be intrusive for the viewer. A popular compromise is placing the watermark in the lower-right corner with enough margin that cropping it would remove important image content.

heading: Adding Watermarks with ToolPic
text: Open ToolPic's Image Watermark tool and upload the images you want to protect. You can process a single image or batch upload multiple files at once. The tool supports JPG, PNG, WebP, and other common image formats.
